summary: Topology-backed map validation passed. The default player-facing shell now resolves through the explicit district-polygon and route-graph topology path, not a hidden rectangular, hex, sequential-id, or regular-lattice authority surface. Map/list/task rail/inspector focus and route-preview reason codes stayed synchronized in the browser path, deterministic path previews and topology hash/save-load behavior remained stable, and focused negative tests covered invalid adjacency, missing route endpoints, blocked routes, disconnected graphs, duplicate capacity use, visual-neighbor-without-route, visually-distant-explicit-route, geometry perturbation invariance, and legacy grid/hex/lattice leakage. Manual smoke review of the 1440x900 and 1280x720 screenshots confirms a readable topology surface with irregular districts, explicit routes, legend copy, and clean console assertions.

manual_smoke.result: Readable irregular district polygons, explicit route line, legend copy, District 1 obligation context, and no rectangle/hex/lattice board.
The smaller viewport still shows topology semantics, route legend, and the same non-grid map shape without a bottom-sliver capture.

tests_run.result: Working tree was clean before writing the REVIEW handoff.
format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check passed.
Vitest and web tests passed. 44 test files, 366 tests, plus 1 web test file and 11 web tests.
Content validation passed for m1, m2, m3, m6, and m7 fixtures.
Node/worker hashes matched for hello, command/query, save/load, M4, M5, and M6 Alpha determinism runs.
Vite production build succeeded with existing chunk-size warnings only.
Storybook build succeeded with existing plugin timing and chunk-size warnings only.
30 Playwright tests passed; screenshot generation and console-clean assertions passed.

tests_run.command: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/content-tools-compiler.test.ts tests/map-renderer-m2-map.test.ts tests/save-load-contract.test.ts tests/sim-core-boot-boundary.test.ts tests/sim-core-m2-route-transport.test.ts
tests_run.result: Focused topology negative tests passed: 5 files, 68 tests.

decisions.decision: Accept the validation conclusion that the default player-facing map is driven by explicit district polygons and route graph topology, not by a hidden row/column, hex axial/cube, sequential-id, or regular-lattice shortcut.
Do not approve formal M7 content lock autonomously; this validation only reopens product-owner reconsideration evidence.

risks.summary: Legacy M2 fixture code still exists for non-default tests, but the default shell path and the verified browser path now resolve through the topology-backed read model.
Route-node markers remain low-fidelity visual dots and are acceptable for current topology validation, but they are not final art.

requested_action: Review the validation evidence, spot-check the screenshots and targeted negative tests if desired, and route onward for task closure if accepted. Do not treat this as content-lock approval.
